core_mechanism: renormalized (resummed) perturbation theory for a weakly nonlinear field, in which nonlinear coupling between Fourier modes damps and phase-shifts the oscillatory features of the field's power/spectrum relative to the linearized prediction
brief_reason: The abstract builds its method on renormalized perturbation theory that resums the perturbative series to capture mode-coupling, which damps the acoustic oscillations and injects out-of-phase oscillations that shift the spectral nodes away from the linear-theory result.
